chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 752 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ اَلثَّوْبُ اَلْوَاحِدُ فِيهِ بَوْلٌ لاَ يَقْدِرُ عَلَى غَسْلِهِ قَالَ «يُصَلِّي فِيهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.752 - Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who has only one garment, and it has urine on it, but he is unable to wash it.
Imam (as) said: "He should pray in it."
